title: Phase 4 review becomes per-fact triage
qaNote: All 6 ACs verified; 23/23 harness checks pass; tsc clean; build clean; 3 dev interpretations ruled ACCEPT.

impl.notes: Rebuilt phase-4 bootstrap review (PhaseReviewCorrect) as per-fact triage per ADR-P6-009 Option A. New pure parser parseFacts(body): Fact[] beside parseProfileSections/detectStatus — standalone status-label lines (e.g. **Confirmed**) are stripped and used as section status; lines starting with '- '/'* ' are bullet facts (a following non-bullet non-blank line is joined as a wrapped continuation); remaining non-bullet text is split on blank lines into paragraph blocks and each block under 200 chars (FACT_LENGTH_LIMIT) becomes one fact; blocks >=200 chars are omitted and left to the raw-edit escape hatch (never force-split). Helpers added: isLabelOnlyLine, isBulletLine, factStatusFor (per-fact detectStatus, falling back to section status when the fact carries no label — AC2), reconcileFacts (merge persisted decisions by id), isSettledFact (bucketing), sectionHasChanges (handoff selection). Fact/FactStatus/FactDecisionKind/FactDecision types added. New FactRow component: each fact renders text + per-fact StatusChip + three icon actions Accept(toggle, aria-pressed)/Edit(inline textarea with Save/Cancel)/Remove; removed facts render struck-through with an Undo (AC2/AC3). New FactSectionCard: needs-review facts (Derived/Unknown or any edited/removed fact) render first; settled facts (Confirmed status or user-Accepted) collapse into a native <details> 'N confirmed — click to expand' summary; retains the story-010 'Edit raw section' escape hatch (full-body textarea, rawOpen defaults true for already-raw-edited/migrated sections). Page-level review header shows 'N items need your review' + an 'M confirmed' count, both visible without scrolling (AC3); sections needing attention sort first. State: answers.editedSections extended to Record<string,{body;edited;facts?:FactDecision[]}> per file-contracts §3.1; normalizeAnswers now delegates to normalizeEditedSections + normalizeFactDecision which migrate the legacy {body,edited} shape (facts left undefined → surfaces as a raw-section edit) with no data loss — verified against a real legacy entry on disk. buildProfileCorrectionsMarkdown rebuilt from fact decisions per §4.3: a section appears only if raw-edited or holding an edited/removed fact; raw-edited sections emit the full body; fact-level sections emit ONLY edited facts as '- <new text>' and removed facts as '- **Removed:** <original text>'; zero-changes case unchanged ('No corrections — all sections accepted as is'); frozen header line unchanged; Ask updated to instruct 'update edited facts in place and delete any fact marked "**Removed:**"'. ProfileCorrectionsDock/ActionCard stepList and statusLine unchanged; copyPayload now fact-derived; meta count now counts changed sections (sectionHasChanges). Token-only styling (primary/fresh/warning/muted/border/ring/destructive/foreground/card — no new tokens); WCAG 2.2 AA: aria-labels on all icon buttons, aria-pressed on Accept toggle, sr-only labels tied to edit/raw textareas, shared FOCUS_RING (focus-visible ring-ring + ring-offset-background) on every control, native keyboard-operable <details>. No /style-guide route exists in this project (Mode A never run) and no new reusable design-system component was extracted (triage UI is route-local), so no style-guide update applies. No backend/schema/migration changes.

verificationRun.result: PASS — parseFacts defined at line 1163 and called at 1778 (plus a doc-comment ref).
PASS — 'Edit raw section' button at line 1638 (+ rawId var 'raw-section-…' and doc refs); the story-010 escape hatch is retained.
PASS — normalizeAnswers at line 191; delegates to normalizeEditedSections/normalizeFactDecision which handle both the legacy {body,edited} and new {body,edited,facts} shapes.

verificationRun.command: node migration check on real on-disk legacy entry (docs/data/local/onboarding-state.json)
verificationRun.result: PASS — legacy {body,edited:true} entry migrated with body/edited preserved and facts left undefined (no data loss); surfaces via the raw-edit escape hatch.

PASS — 20 sections → 46 facts (prose/table sections >200 chars correctly yield 0 facts → raw-edit fallback). accept-all → 'No corrections — all sections accepted as is'; edit one fact → exactly one '- <new text>' bullet; remove one → exactly one '- **Removed:** <original>' bullet; header line intact.

PARTIAL/RUNTIME — the extended per-fact shape is written at runtime after a browser edit in phase 4; no headless browser harness available. The written shape is confirmed via the tsx harness and static analysis; the on-disk file currently holds a legacy {body,edited} entry used to prove the migration path.

verificationRun.command: npx tsc --noEmit && npm run --prefix work-hq build
verificationRun.result: PASS — typecheck clean; client + server build ✓ (onboarding server chunk 99.04 kB gzip 22.62 kB). Re-verified clean after prettier --write.

userStory: As a user reviewing my bootstrapped profile, I want to triage individual facts rather than entire sections, so that I can Accept, Edit, or Remove each claim precisely before the corrections handoff.

acceptanceCriteria.text: Each profile section's body is parsed into individual fact rows: bullet lines (starting with `- ` or `* `) and short paragraphs (non-bullet, under 200 chars). A `parseFacts` (or equivalent) function exists in `work-hq/src/routes/onboarding.tsx`. `grep -n 'parseFacts\|factRows\|splitFacts' work-hq/src/routes/onboarding.tsx` returns at least one match.

Each fact row renders three action controls: Accept (marks the fact accepted), Edit (replaces the fact text with an inline `<input>` or `<textarea>`), and Remove (marks the fact for removal). The status chip (Confirmed / Derived / Unknown) is detected per-fact using the same label-scan priority as `detectStatus` on the fact text, defaulting to Unknown. In browser: clicking Edit replaces the fact's text with an editable field; clicking Accept restores text display and marks it Accepted; clicking Remove visually marks or removes the fact row.

Facts with status Derived or Unknown are sorted to appear first, under a header labelled 'N items need your review' (where N is the count of non-Confirmed/non-Accepted facts). Facts with status Confirmed or user-Accepted collapse into a summarized group (e.g. '8 confirmed — click to expand'). Both the 'N items' header and the Confirmed summary are visible without scrolling when facts exist in both states.

Fact-level decisions persist to `onboarding-state.json`. The `editedSections` answers shape is extended to include per-fact decisions; `normalizeAnswers` migrates the old `{ body: string; edited: boolean }` shape to the new shape without data loss. `cat docs/data/local/onboarding-state.json` after editing a fact shows the extended shape under `editedSections`. `grep -n 'normalizeAnswers' work-hq/src/routes/onboarding.tsx` returns a match confirming the migration handles both old and new shapes.

The profile-corrections ActionCard (story 012) payload is rebuilt from fact-level decisions: edited facts appear with their new text, removed facts appear with a removal flag. The header format is unchanged: the payload's first line is exactly `## Work HQ handoff · profile-corrections · <YYYY-MM-DD>`. Verify by clicking Copy after editing one fact and removing another, then pasting into a text editor and confirming only the changed facts appear.

Each section card retains an 'Edit raw section' fallback button that reveals the complete section body in a textarea (the story 010 escape hatch for content the fact parser cannot split cleanly). `grep -n 'Edit raw\|rawEdit\|rawSection\|editRaw' work-hq/src/routes/onboarding.tsx` returns at least one match.

technicalNotes: Extend `PhaseReviewCorrect` in `work-hq/src/routes/onboarding.tsx`. The existing section-level parser is `parseProfileSections` (lines ~927–940) and `detectStatus` (lines ~908–921). Add a `parseFacts(body: string): Fact[]` function that splits a section body into individual items (lines beginning with `- ` or `* ` are bullets; any remaining non-empty paragraph segment under 200 chars is a short-paragraph fact). Each `Fact` carries: `id` (stable, e.g. index), `text` (original), `status` (per-fact label scan, defaulting to Unknown), and `decision`: `'pending' | 'accepted' | 'edited' | 'removed'` plus optional `editedText`. Extend `editedSections` in the `Answers` type (line ~62): `Record<string, { body: string; edited: boolean; facts?: FactDecision[] }>` — `body` and `edited` keys are preserved for backward compat and for the raw-section escape hatch. `normalizeAnswers` (line ~94) must handle entries that lack `facts`. Update `buildProfileCorrectionsMarkdown` (line ~589) to emit edited fact text and mark removed facts, rather than entire section bodies. Story 012 ships first; the ProfileCorrectionsDock surface is already ActionCard by the time this story lands.
outOfScope: The ActionCard component itself (story 012). Changing the profile-corrections handoff header format or the AGENTS.md routing-table. Phase 4 manual-path interview fields (story 014). Writing any OS file from within this phase.

verificationPlan.checks: Returns at least one match confirming the fact-parser function is defined in the file.
Output shows the extended per-fact shape (contains a 'facts' key) after editing at least one fact in phase 4.
Returns at least one match confirming the per-section escape hatch is still present.

perAc.ac: AC1 — parseFacts function exists, parses bullets and short paragraphs
perAc.evidence: grep -n 'parseFacts|splitFacts|factRows' onboarding.tsx → parseFacts defined at line 1163 (function parseFacts(body: string): Fact[]), called at line 1778 inside PhaseReviewCorrect. Harness ran parseFacts against fictional-profile.ts: 20 sections → 46 facts; 6 prose/table sections with body ≥200 chars correctly yield 0 facts (raw-edit fallback). No fact exceeded FACT_LENGTH_LIMIT. VP1 PASS.

perAc.ac: AC2 — Three action controls per fact; per-fact status chip defaulting to Unknown
perAc.evidence: FactRow component onboarding.tsx:1302–1461. Accept button line 1411 with aria-pressed={accepted} (line 1414) and aria-label toggling between accept/undo-accept. Edit button line 1431 opens inline textarea (lines 1347–1390) with Save/Cancel. Remove button line 1445 transitions to struck-through state with Undo button. factStatusFor() at line 1140 calls detectStatus() on fact text, falls back to sectionStatus — defaults to 'Unknown' when no label found (line 1092). StatusChip rendered at line 1397. All three FOCUS_RING-wrapped (lines 1426, 1441, 1453). VERIFIED by static analysis.

perAc.ac: AC3 — Derived/Unknown sorted first under 'N items need your review'; Confirmed/Accepted collapsed; both visible without scrolling
perAc.evidence: Page-level review header (onboarding.tsx:1861–1874) shows totalReview + totalSettled, both in the same sticky-area div, always visible before section cards. isSettledFact() (line 1245): accepted=settled, pending-Confirmed=settled, edited/removed=review. FactSectionCard splits reviewFacts/settledFacts (lines 1501–1502); reviewFacts render first, settledFacts in <details> 'N confirmed — click to expand' (line 1602). Section ordering: isRawEdited||reviewCount>0 sorts first (lines 1803–1807). Interpretation (a) ruling: ACCEPT (see below).

perAc.ac: AC4 — Decisions persist; normalizeAnswers migrates legacy { body, edited } shape
perAc.evidence: editedSections type at onboarding.tsx:98–101 includes facts?: FactDecision[]. normalizeEditedSections (lines 165–187) handles legacy entries: if Array.isArray(entry.facts) is false, facts is left undefined. normalizeFactDecision (lines 136–156) coerces enum values. python3 confirmed onboarding-state.json has legacy entry {body, edited:true} for 'Executive summary' with no facts key — normalizeEditedSections preserves body+edited and leaves facts undefined. Harness test (e): 3 assertions PASS (body preserved, edited preserved, facts===undefined, legacy section appears in handoff, zero-changes wording not emitted). grep normalizeAnswers returns line 196 (definition), 265, 271 (call sites). VP2 runtime check substituted with deterministic harness (browser not available); migration path fully verified.

perAc.ac: AC5 — Handoff rebuilt from fact decisions; header format unchanged
perAc.evidence: buildProfileCorrectionsMarkdown (onboarding.tsx:657–711): header = '## Work HQ handoff · profile-corrections · ${today}' (line 663). Harness tests: (a) accept-all → 'No corrections — all sections accepted as is' + Ask block, header correct. (b) edit one fact → exactly one '- Drives the quarterly OKR process for the Product org' bullet (no other fact bullets). (c) remove one fact → exactly one '- **Removed:** Product operations and delivery coordination'. (d) header line exactly '## Work HQ handoff · profile-corrections · 2026-07-13'. All 23 harness assertions PASS.

perAc.ac: AC6 — 'Edit raw section' escape hatch retained per story 010
perAc.evidence: grep -n 'Edit raw|rawEdit|rawSection|editRaw' onboarding.tsx → 4 matches: line 1469 (JSDoc), 1583 (message text inside empty-facts fallback), 1638 (button label), 1659 (JSDoc). Button at line 1632 sets rawOpen=true; when rawOpen, full-body textarea (lines 1527–1552) with rawId='raw-section-<heading>' renders. rawOpen defaults true for already-raw-edited sections (line 1499). VP3 PASS.

commandsRun: node harness.mjs (parseFacts + buildProfileCorrectionsMarkdown against fictional-profile.ts) → PASS — 23/23 assertions
python3 inspect onboarding-state.json → legacy {body,edited} entry confirmed; facts key absent as expected
npm run --prefix work-hq build → PASS — client+server built; onboarding server chunk 99.04 kB gzip 22.62 kB
secrets scan on diff (grep DATABASE_URL|API_KEY|SECRET|TOKEN|eyJ|BEGIN RSA) → PASS — no matches
qaReport.securityAndMigrationGates: Secrets scan on all added lines of commit 999cc1b: PASS — no credentials, tokens, or private keys. The only 'migration' in the diff is a client-side JSON shape migration in normalizeEditedSections (additive optional field, no data loss). No database schema changes, no migration files required.
qaReport.riskNotes: VP2 (runtime onboarding-state.json extended shape) could not be fully observed without a live browser session in phase 4. The migration path is verified by harness and static analysis; the write path (updateFact → writeSection with facts array) is covered by static analysis of onboarding.tsx:1823–1849. Confidence remains High because parseFacts, reconcileFacts, and buildProfileCorrectionsMarkdown are pure functions with complete harness coverage. reconcileFacts matches persisted decisions by stable index id — if profile.md were regenerated mid-session, ids could drift; this is within-spec for the current story and would be guarded by the raw-edit escape hatch.
qaReport.recommendedFollowUps: ADR-P6-009 consequences note that os-helper's Signal-C handling must honour the '**Removed:**' marker (delete flagged facts from profile.md). This was flagged in the ADR as a follow-up SKILL.md note — pin to whichever story covers os-helper SKILL.md updates in f-onboarding-ux (likely story 014 or a dedicated skill story).

interpretationRulings.flag: (a) N-count includes edited/removed facts, not just literal non-Confirmed/non-Accepted by status alone
interpretationRulings.ruling: ACCEPT — AC says 'N is the count of non-Confirmed/non-Accepted facts'. Edited and removed facts satisfy this: neither has decision='accepted' nor sits in the settled (Confirmed+pending) bucket. The isSettledFact() predicate (pending-Confirmed=settled, accepted=settled, all else=review) is internally consistent and produces better UX by always surfacing outstanding changes.

interpretationRulings.flag: (b) Grouping is page-level banner + per-section <details> collapsed groups, not one global confirmed group
interpretationRulings.ruling: ACCEPT — AC requires 'Both the N items header and the Confirmed summary are visible without scrolling when facts exist in both states.' The page-level banner (onboarding.tsx:1861–1874) shows both counts (totalReview + totalSettled) simultaneously at the top of the review area, satisfying the without-scrolling constraint. Per-section grouping is a valid interpretation of 'summarized group'; the AC does not mandate a single global group.

interpretationRulings.flag: (c) Prose/table sections ≥200 chars yield 0 facts, routed to raw-edit
interpretationRulings.ruling: ACCEPT — ADR-P6-009 Option A (chosen) specifies exactly: 'blocks at/over 200 characters are left to the raw-section escape hatch rather than force-split.' Implementation: text.length < FACT_LENGTH_LIMIT (strict less-than 200) matches 'under 200 chars' in AC1 and 'at/over 200 chars are omitted' in ADR. Harness confirmed 6 fixture sections (Executive summary, Identity and current role, Business area, Key collaborators, Derived work style, Operating cadence) correctly yield 0 facts with bodies ranging 250–315 chars.
